top_signals.title: UN estimates 80 mines remain in Hormuz
top_signals.summary: The United Nations’ International Maritime Organization said Friday that approximately 80 mines remain in historic shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting the challenge of restoring normal operations in the critical oil transit route. The Iran war created two alternative shipping channels through Hormuz. One route follows Oman’s coast and is coordinated by the ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Surge in Asia-US container rates continues, tanker rates fall as Strait of Hormuz transits rise
top_signals.summary: Rates for shipping containers from east Asia and China to the US continued to surge as importers pull volumes forward ahead of possible new tariffs, while liquid tanker rates continued to soften amid a tentative ceasefire in the Middle East. top_signals.title: Tanker Market Now a “Hostage” of Geopolitics
top_signals.summary: The tanker market has felt the full force of geopolitical factors having an impact on freight rates, demand and cargo flows/ton-mile demand, since the start of 2025. top_signals.title: Wärtsilä warns of hurdles for low-carbon bunker use amid IMO delays
top_signals.summary: Shipping companies are facing greater challenges in transitioning to low-carbon fuels amid an uncertain, fragmented regulatory outlook following the International Maritime Organization’s failure to adopt a global decarbonization framework, according to the top marine executive of Finnish technology group Wärtsilä. The UN agency in October 2025 voted to delay the discussions of the adoption of ...

top_signals.title: Scorpio Tankers resumes Persian Gulf transit; LR2 to deliver jet fuel to Europe for $10 million
top_signals.summary: Scorpio Tankers, the world’s largest LR2 shipping company by fleet size, has commenced transiting through the Strait of Hormuz, with one of its LR2s chartered by Admic for $10 million to deliver jet fuel in Europe, three sources with tanker brokerages and charterers said June 25. top_signals.title: War, Weather & Tragedy: Why Coal Demand Could Surge in 2026
top_signals.summary: Global coal demand has been rising over the last couple of years, with new record highs reached in both 2024 and 2025 at around 8.8 billion tonnes, according to the International Energy Agency (IEA). However, growth has slowed and speculation regarding “peak coal” has emerged as the world continues to decarbonize. Advanced economies are phasing ...

top_signals.title: The World's Biggest Energy Bet Is No Longer on Fossil Fuels
top_signals.summary: For years, critics of the energy transition have made essentially the same argument. Renewable energy was supposedly too expensive, too dependent on subsidies, too intermittent, and too vulnerable to survive a serious energy security crisis. Sooner or later, they argued, governments and investors would return to the comfort of oil, gas, and coal. The latest figures from the International Energy Agency suggest the opposite is happening. According to the IEA's newly released World Energy Investment 2026 report, global investment in clean energy has…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: VLCCs Down Last Week
top_signals.summary: Clean LR2 The TC1 75kt MEG/Japan index climbed 18.88 points this week to WS509.44. A voyage west also freighted up this week with the TC20 90kt MEG/UK-Continent index going from $9.38 million to $9.93 million. top_signals.title: Burnham to unveil sweeping plans for devolution and ‘reindustrialisation’
top_signals.summary: Andy Burnham will unveil sweeping plans to devolve power away from London and shift some government operations to a “Number 10 in the North”, as the Prime Minister-to-be calls for a change in “how Britain is governed”.
